rows.definition: The scorecard that measures how easy fak is for an AI agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, an MCP client) to discover, adopt, and build on - missing entry points, harness configs, copy-pasteable first commands, per-agent recipes (tools/agent_readiness_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: The INWARD agent-experience surface - NOT persona-readiness (the go-to-market human personas) and NOT the control pane that folds every scorecard.

rows.definition: The scorecard that measures whether fak serves its top human personas - the free-tier dev, the infra engineer, the researcher - each with the entry path they need (prebuilt binary, deploy guide, determinism witness) (tools/persona_readiness_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: The go-to-market HUMAN-persona surface - NOT agent-readiness (the single AI-agent persona) and NOT the control pane.

rows.definition: The scorecard that measures whether a reader-facing doc reads like a person wrote it: em-dash flood, bold-emphasis flood, run-on sentences, walls of text, LLM-scaffolding phrases (tools/doc_appeal_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: The prose-VOICE surface (does it sound human) - NOT docs-scorecard (whole-corpus doc coverage) and NOT readme-freshness (is the front page current).

rows.definition: The whole-corpus documentation scorecard: the 5-KPI doc-debt measure over the reachable doc set, the corpus analogue of the README freshness audit (tools/docs_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: The whole-corpus doc-COVERAGE surface - NOT doc-appeal (per-doc prose voice) and NOT readme-freshness (the single front-page doc).

rows.definition: The scorecard that measures observability debt across the metric, dashboard, alert, trace, log, proof, and DOS surfaces - what is and is not measured about the running system (tools/observability_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: Measures WHAT-IS-MEASURED about the system - NOT conflation (whose number a metric reports) and NOT the control pane that folds the scorecards.

rows.definition: The scorecard that measures how steerable fak is - whether its behavior responds predictably to the configured knobs, flags, and policies (tools/steerability_scorecard.py, cmd/fak steering).
rows.distinction: Measures BEHAVIORAL-RESPONSE-TO-KNOBS - NOT token-defaults (whether the shipped defaults are sane) and NOT the control pane.

rows.definition: The scorecard that measures whether the project is ready to cut a release: version state, release-notes draft, green gate, and the substantive-commit threshold the auto-cut decision reads (tools/release_readiness_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: Measures RELEASE-CUT readiness - NOT build-scorecard (the build/onboard process health) and NOT the control pane.

rows.definition: The scorecard that measures search-engine and answer-engine optimization debt: structured-data sync, FAQ coverage, and the discoverability content checks over the public surfaces (tools/seo_aeo_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: Measures DISCOVERABILITY by search/answer engines - NOT industry-parity (the competitive field position) and NOT the control pane.

rows.definition: The scorecard that measures the developer experience of running benchmarks: whether the bench grid is discoverable, reproducible, and well-documented (tools/bench_dx_scorecard.py).
rows.distinction: Measures the BENCHMARK developer experience (process debt, free to regress vs the ratchet) - NOT industry-parity (the measured competitive numbers) and NOT the control pane.

rows.definition: The scorecard that grades the CUDA build/dev process health (standing author, build, validate, gate, onboard) folded into the guard-RSI and cuda-dev surfaces (internal/guardrsi buildScorecard).
rows.distinction: Grades the BUILD/DEV PROCESS - NOT code-quality (defects in the source) and NOT the control pane that folds every scorecard.

rows.definition: The single debt integer the repo-hygiene scorecard folds to: noisy artifacts, placement drift, generated junk across the tree.
rows.distinction: One debt INTEGER for tree cleanliness - NOT the repo-hygiene scorecard subsystem that computes it, and NOT total-debt (the portfolio sum across all scorecards).

rows.definition: The single bounded debt integer emitted by the operator-heaviness scorecard: live operator pressure folded into the control pane by `fak operator heaviness`.
rows.distinction: One debt INTEGER for operator-heaviness pressure - NOT the operator-heaviness scorecard subsystem and NOT total-debt (the portfolio sum across all scorecards).

rows.definition: gardenbundle.PlanTick(results, dryRun) computes a TickPlan - the decided set of stale-work reap and surface actions for one garden tick from the member results.
rows.distinction: The PLANNING step that DECIDES a garden tick's actions - NOT the TickPlan value it returns, and NOT a dispatch wave or lane plan.

rows.definition: gardenbundle.TickPlan is the value PlanTick returns: the decided reap/surface counts for one garden tick, with Acted() true when the tick will change anything.
rows.distinction: The decided-actions VALUE for one garden tick - NOT PlanTick (the function that computes it) and NOT a dispatch plan.

rows.definition: dispatchtick.LaneCandidate is one orderable issue in a dispatch lane: its number and the priority signal orderLaneCandidates ranks it by.
rows.distinction: One candidate ISSUE within a single lane's ordering - NOT a dispatchWaveCandidate (a lane chosen to run in a wave) and NOT the whole dispatchPlan.

rows.definition: cmd/fak dispatchWaveCandidate is one lane chosen to run in a dispatch wave - a RunTarget in the wave price, the lane-level unit the wave admits under disjointness.
rows.distinction: One LANE selected for a wave's RunTargets - NOT a LaneCandidate (an issue inside a lane) and NOT the dispatchPlan that scores the whole dispatch surface.
